overview: Compares source text between two or three document versions to leverage existing translations for new source content. Supports both bilingual (old source + old translation in one file) and trilingual (separate old source and old translation files) input modes. Paragraphs (TextUnits) must be aligned across all input documents.

fuzzyThreshold.help:
Controls the minimum match quality required to leverage an old translation. A score of **100** means only exact matches (both text and inline codes) are leveraged. Lower values allow fuzzy matches — e.g., setting to **95** will leverage translations where the source has minor differences from the old version.

When a match score falls below this threshold, the old target text is ignored entirely.
fuzzyThreshold.values: Integer from 1 to 100. **100** = exact match only. Lower values = more permissive fuzzy matching.

codesensitive.help:
When enabled, inline codes (formatting tags, placeholders) are included in the comparison algorithm. This makes matching stricter — segments that differ only in their inline codes will score lower.

Disabling this compares only the textual content, ignoring any inline code differences. Useful when code positions or types frequently change between versions but the text remains the same.

dependsOn.condition: Only meaningful when fuzzyThreshold < 100; at 100, code differences already prevent a match

diffOnly.help:
Runs the comparison without copying translations or creating leverage annotations. The TextUnit is simply marked as matched or not.

Useful for analysis or reporting — lets you see which segments changed between document versions without actually applying any leveraged translations.

dependsOn.condition: conflicts with copyToTarget — when diffOnly is true, no content is copied regardless of copyToTarget

copyToTarget.help:
Copies the leveraged old translation directly into the target content. A leverage annotation is still created.

**Use with caution:** the copied target will **not** be segmented, and any existing target content will be **overwritten** and lost.
notes: **Warning:** Copied target will not be segmented. If downstream steps expect segmented content, this may cause issues.
**Warning:** Any existing target content is permanently overwritten when a match is found.

dependsOn.condition: conflicts with diffOnly — has no effect when diffOnly is true

limitations: The comparison process is done **in memory** — very large documents may cause memory issues.
TextUnits must be aligned (same number, same order) across all input documents. Misaligned documents will produce incorrect leverage results.
processingNotes: Accepts two input modes: **bilingual** (new source + bilingual old document) or **trilingual** (new source + old source + old translation as separate files).
When using three documents, the second (old source) and third (old translation) must have matching TextUnit counts and order.

examples.title: Bilingual leverage (two inputs)
examples.description: The new document is compared against a bilingual file containing both old source and old translation. Matched segments above the threshold get their old translations leveraged.

examples.title: Trilingual leverage (three inputs)
examples.description: The new document is compared against a separate old source file and a separate old translation file. The old source and old translation files must have aligned TextUnits (same count, same order).
